title: calceolaria triphylla
intro: Calceolaria triphylla, also known as slipper flower or pocket flower, is a type of flowering plant native to South America, belonging to the Calceolaria genus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Calceolaria triphylla include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Calceolaria triphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, with the plant's leaves and flowers being used in these preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
